Canonical reference for contract termination clauses, jurisdiction, and settlement language.

## Termination

Termination clauses define the conditions under which a contract ends. Standard categories: for-cause, for-convenience, mutual, and automatic expiry.

### For-cause termination

Triggered by a defined breach. The non-breaching party typically issues written notice and a cure period.

### For-convenience termination

Either party may end the contract with notice, regardless of breach. Common in service agreements.

## Jurisdiction

Specifies the governing law and venue for disputes. Choice-of-law and forum-selection clauses usually appear together.
